title: Xeriscape Spring Kenyan Bulgur Pilaf
description: Umami and springy Kenyan dish featuring bulgur wheat, ideal for a romantic dinner on a robata grill. This whole30-friendly pilaf is a flavorful twist on traditional bulgur recipes.

ingredients.name: bulgur wheat
ingredients.notes: preferably Middle Eastern or Turkish in origin

instructions: In a medium saucepan, combine chicken bouillon and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, in a separate saucepan, heat coconut oil over medium heat.
Add onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add garlic, ginger, lemongrass, turmeric, paprika, salt, and pepper. Cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Add bulgur wheat and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly.
Remove from heat and cover with a lid.
In a separate pot, boil water and ice together for an ice bath.
Once chicken bouillon has finished simmering, remove from heat and carefully pour into the ice bath to stop cooking.
Strain chicken bouillon through a fine-mesh sieve and discard solids.
Add strained chicken bouillon to the saucepan with bulgur wheat and stir to combine.
Add chicken breast to the saucepan and simmer until cooked through, about 10 minutes.
Remove chicken breast from the saucepan and let cool.
Once cooled, cut chicken into bite-sized pieces and add to the saucepan with bulgur wheat.
Stir in chopped cilantro and serve hot.
To serve, place pilaf on a robata grill and cook until lightly charred, about 5 minutes per side.
Serve immediately and garnish with additional chopped cilantro if desired.
